Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 264 upvotes, 24 comments
  2. Zipp 2001 Restoration 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 53 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 30 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Rack-mount hydroponics 313 upvotes, 83 comments
  6. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 35 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. LLM Architecture Gallery 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 60 upvotes, 14 comments
  9. Kniterate Notes 43 upvotes, 10 comments
  10. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 90 upvotes, 27 comments
  11. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 64 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 130 upvotes, 48 comments
  13. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 193 upvotes, 77 comments
  14. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 280 upvotes, 133 comments
  16. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 97 upvotes, 43 comments
  17. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 46 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 135 upvotes, 72 comments
  19. Grandparents are glued to their phones, families are worried [video] 106 upvotes, 60 comments
  20. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 53 upvotes, 27 comments
  21. Office.eu launches as Europe's sovereign office platform 102 upvotes, 66 comments
  22. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 68 upvotes, 41 comments
  23. How kernel anti-cheats work 321 upvotes, 276 comments
  24. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 116 upvotes, 89 comments
  25. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 317 upvotes, 291 comments
  26. Let your Coding Agent debug the browser session with Chrome DevTools MCP 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 184 upvotes, 241 comments
  28. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 156 upvotes, 214 comments
  29. Autoresearch Hub 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 69 upvotes, 211 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
